formulas.name: Journal
formulas.notes: A journal is a book in which transactions are recorded chronologically.
formulas.formula: The first entry in the accounting cycle that contains a summary of all the business transactions in a chronological order.

formulas.name: Ledger
formulas.notes: A ledger is a book in which all the accounts of a business are maintained.
formulas.formula: The book that contains the permanent records of all the accounts of a business.

formulas.name: Account
formulas.notes: An account is a record of the financial effects of a business transaction.
formulas.formula: A record of the financial effects of a business transaction.

formulas.name: Assets = Liabilities + Capital
formulas.notes: This formula is based on the accounting equation and is used to determine the amount of capital in a business.

variables.Assets: The total value of a business's resources.
variables.Capital: The owner's claim on the business.
variables.Liabilities: The total claims of outsiders against a business.

formulas.name: Accrual Concept
formulas.notes: Accrual concept is a matching concept where expenses are matched with the revenue they help to generate.
formulas.formula: Expenses are matched with the revenue they help to generate.

formulas.name: Matching Concept
formulas.notes: Matching concept is a concept where expenses are matched with the revenue they help to generate.
formulas.formula: Expenses are matched with the revenue they help to generate.

formulas.name: Going Concern Concept
formulas.notes: Going concern concept is a concept where a business is assumed to continue operating for the foreseeable future.
formulas.formula: A business will continue to operate for the foreseeable future.

formulas.name: Monetary Unit Concept
formulas.notes: Monetary unit concept is a concept where all transactions are recorded in money terms.
formulas.formula: All transactions are recorded in money terms.

formulas.name: Historical Cost Concept
formulas.notes: Historical cost concept is a concept where assets and liabilities are recorded at their original cost.
formulas.formula: Assets and liabilities are recorded at their original cost.

formulas.name: Entity Concept
formulas.notes: Entity concept is a concept where a business is treated as a separate entity from its owner.
formulas.formula: A business is treated as a separate entity from its owner.
